title: Consumer/partner outreach + baseline time-to-find measurement
context: OER are abundant and free but are not indexed against the curriculum standards that govern what teachers must teach, so teachers cannot find the free resource aligned to a given standard. This project builds an open crosswalk of educator-reviewed AlignmentRecords linking OER to standards by stable identifier (CASE GUID / ASN URI), not by reproducing copyrighted standards text. Two hard gates never skip: the dual licence/terms gate (gate-002) and educator review of every record, with a blind second-reviewer precision audit holding precision at >= 95%. Outcomes are measured by real reuse; this task opens partner conversations and captures the time-to-find baseline.
objective: Open consumer/partner outreach and measure the baseline time-to-find an aligned OER for a given standard.
acceptanceCriteria: Identifies and contacts candidate consuming partners (repositories/districts/platforms); at least one outreach thread opened and recorded.
Baseline time-to-find measured with a documented method and sample, to compare against later usability tests.
No PII retained beyond what is needed; results recorded for the outcome ledger.
Output licensed CC-BY-4.0; the commit is DCO signed-off.
